channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After Trump's re-election, these U.S. scientists found jobs in the U.K.
item.description: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
item.content:encoded: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Inside the coordinated strategy to radically reshape U.S. immigration
item.description: As the Supreme Court today weighs the Trump administration's effort to revoke birthright citizenship, NPR looks at what else the White House has done to curb illegal and legal migration.
item.content:encoded: As the Supreme Court today weighs the Trump administration's effort to revoke birthright citizenship, NPR looks at what else the White House has done to curb illegal and legal migration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Here are Colorado's 2026 primary election results
item.description: Live election results: Get the latest on Colorado's U.S. Senate, U.S. House and gubernatorial primary races.
item.content:encoded: Live election results: Get the latest on Colorado's U.S. Senate, U.S. House and gubernatorial primary races.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: U.S. murder rate approaches a record low
item.description: A few years ago, experts worried about a "new normal" of elevated violent crime in the U.S. Now the country is flirting with breaking its all-time low murder rate
item.content:encoded:
A few years ago, experts worried about a "new normal" of elevated violent crime in the U.S. Now the country is flirting with breaking its all-time low murder rate

(Image credit: Mark Stockwell)
item.dc:creator: Martin Kaste

item.title: Venezuelans deported from the U.S. were killed hours later in powerful quakes
item.description: On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.
item.content:encoded:
On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.

(Image credit: Matias Delacroix)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Federal money for workforce training begins, but few programs qualify
item.description: July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. So far just 12 states have created roadmaps for colleges to apply.
item.content:encoded:
July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. So far just 12 states have created roadmaps for colleges to apply.

(Image credit: Annelise Capossela for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The U.S. and Iran will resume peace talks Tuesday, SCOTUS expected to make a decision on birthright citizenship, Colorado voters head to the polls.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Iran will resume peace talks Tuesday, SCOTUS expected to make a decision on birthright citizenship, Colorado voters head to the polls.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Explosion in Monaco injures 3, including Ukrainian tycoon
item.description: Monaco 's chief prosecutor said the suspect who placed an explosive device that injured three people, including a reported Ukrainian tycoon, acted alone and remains at large.
item.content:encoded:
Monaco 's chief prosecutor said the suspect who placed an explosive device that injured three people, including a reported Ukrainian tycoon, acted alone and remains at large.

(Image credit: Philippe Magoni)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Chinese billionaire Guo Wengui gets 30 years in U.S. prison for fraud conviction
item.description: Guo said he came to the U.S. to destroy the Chinese Communist Party. But the judge said he instead diverted investor money to live lavishly.
item.content:encoded:
Guo said he came to the U.S. to destroy the Chinese Communist Party. But the judge said he instead diverted investor money to live lavishly.

(Image credit: Elizabeth Williams)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Michigan governor threatens to pull troops from D.C. if used for Trump task force
item.description: Michigan Gov. Whitmer is one of four Democrats who sent their states' National Guard troops to Washington, D.C. ahead of America 250 celebrations in recent weeks, amid President Trump's ongoing — and controversial — deployment in the city.
item.content:encoded:
Michigan Gov. Whitmer is one of four Democrats who sent their states' National Guard troops to Washington, D.C. ahead of America 250 celebrations in recent weeks, amid President Trump's ongoing — and controversial — deployment in the city.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf
